Sunday, April 26, 2026 Daily Digest

Google's $40B Anthropic Bet, Ulta + Gemini Go Agentic, CrowdStrike Secures the AI Agent Era

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Google Plans $40B Investment in Anthropic Despite Gemini Rivalry

In a move that redefines "coopetition," Google is reportedly preparing to invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic — maker of Claude, Gemini's direct competitor. The deal values Anthropic at $350B, with $10B upfront and $30B tied to milestones. The message: in AI, controlling the infrastructure layer matters more than winning every product battle.

Read on International News →
2

Ulta Partners with Google Gemini for Agentic Beauty Shopping

Ulta is embedding Gemini directly into its website and app, plus extending its 30,000-product catalog across Google Search, Shopping, and Gemini's discovery platform. The goal: intercept the 30-45% of consumers now using AI agents for shopping research before they reach a competitor. This is agentic commerce in action — and a direct shot at Sephora.

Read on Forbes →
3

CrowdStrike Launches AI Agent Security Suite

CrowdStrike is positioning itself as the security layer for the agentic AI era. New capabilities include Shadow AI Discovery (1,800+ unauthorized AI apps found across customers), AI Detection and Response for prompt interception, and Charlotte AI AgentWorks — an open platform for building secure agents with Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS, and others. The framing: "You're not securing software. You're securing autonomous behaviors."

Read on Forbes →
4

Microsoft's $25B Australia AI Spend Signals Infrastructure Wars

Microsoft's massive Australian AI commitment is thin on detail but heavy on implication: infrastructure is becoming the battleground. For B2B marketers, the key insight is that the buyer journey is now AI-mediated — buyers arrive with better questions and filter out generic messaging earlier. Your content needs to be machine-readable, not just brand-friendly.

Read on Big Wave Digital →
5

Cloudflare Expands Agent Cloud with GPT-5.4 Integration

Cloudflare is challenging containers as the default runtime for enterprise AI agents with its expanded Agent Cloud platform. The GPT-5.4 integration and isolate-based "Dynamic Workers" signal a shift: AI agents need purpose-built infrastructure, not retrofitted cloud architectures. The edge is becoming the execution layer for agentic AI.

Read on Forbes →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Ulta + Google partnership isn't just a retail story — it's a template for how every enterprise should be thinking. McKinsey warns that AI agents are "reshaping how intent forms." If 30-45% of consumers are using AI for purchase research, what's the B2B number? Companies that optimize for machine-mediated discovery will win. Those still building for human-first landing pages will wonder where their pipeline went.
